content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 19 — SOPHIA Consensus Translation

**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Sources:** EMET (literal), LOGOS (formal), PNEUMA (dynamic) | **Chapter:** Deuteronomy 19 (21 verses) | **LC:** pp. 216–217 | **Vaticanus:** p. 155

---

**1** When the LORD your God cuts off the nations whose land the LORD your God is giving you, and you dispossess them and settle in their cities and in their houses, **2** you shall set apart three cities for yourself in the midst of your land that the LORD your God is giving you to possess. **3** You shall prepare the road and divide into three parts the territory of your land that the LORD your God gives you as an inheritance, so that any manslayer may flee there.

**4** And this is the case of the manslayer who shall flee there and live: whoever strikes his neighbor without knowledge, and did not hate him from yesterday or the day before— **5** as when a man goes into the forest with his neighbor to cut wood, and his hand swings the axe to fell a tree, and the iron head slips from the wood and strikes his neighbor so that he dies—he shall flee to one of these cities and live, **6** lest the avenger of blood pursue the manslayer while his heart is hot and overtake him, because the way is long, and strike him fatally—though he was not deserving of death, since he did not hate him from yesterday or the day before. **7** Therefore I am commanding you: You shall set apart three cities for yourself.

**8** And if the LORD your God enlarges your territory, as he swore to your fathers, and gives you all the land that he promised to give to your fathers— **9** provided that you keep all this commandment that I am commanding you today, to love the LORD your God and to walk in his ways always—then you shall add three more cities for yourself beyond these three, **10** so that innocent blood may not be shed in the midst of your land that the LORD your God is giving you as an inheritance, and blood-guilt come upon you.

**11** But if a man hates his neighbor and lies in wait for him and rises against him and strikes him fatally so that he dies, and he flees to one of these cities— **12** then the elders of his city shall send and take him from there and hand him over to the avenger of blood, so that he may die. **13** Your eye shall not pity him; you shall purge the guilt of innocent blood from Israel, and it will go well for you.

**14** You shall not move your neighbor's boundary marker, which the former generations have set, in your inheritance that you will inherit in the land that the LORD your God is giving you to possess.

**15** A single witness shall not rise against a man for any iniquity or for any sin, in connection with any sin that he commits. On the evidence of two witnesses or on the evidence of three witnesses a matter shall be established. **16** If a malicious witness rises against a man to testify against him of wrongdoing, **17** then the two men who have the dispute shall stand before the LORD, before the priests and the judges who are in office in those days. **18** And the judges shall inquire diligently, and if the witness is a false witness and has testified falsely against his brother— **19** then you shall do to him as he intended to do to his brother. So you shall purge the evil from your midst. **20** And those who remain shall hear and fear, and shall never again commit such an evil thing in your midst. **21** And your eye shall not pity: life for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot.

---

## SOPHIA's Council Notes

**Chapter overview.** Deuteronomy 19 gathers three distinct legal spheres: the cities of refuge (vv. 1–13), the boundary-marker law (v. 14), and the witness laws with their lex talionis conclusion (vv. 15–21). The three agents converge closely throughout. Key arbitration points are noted below.

**v.1 "settle / dwell."** EMET: "dwell." LOGOS: "settle." PNEUMA: "settle." Consensus adopts "settle" as the more natural English complement to "cities and houses."

**v.3 "prepare the road" vs. "prepare for yourself the way."** All three render the road-preparation instruction. Consensus follows LOGOS/PNEUMA in removing the reflexive "for yourself" from v.3's road clause (it clusters awkwardly with the "for yourself" of v.2), preserving readability without loss of meaning.

**v.4–6 Idiom "from yesterday or the day before."** All three preserve the literal Hebrew idiom *mitmol shilshom*, the standard Deuteronomic formula for prior enmity. Consensus retains it; the phrase is sufficiently transparent and carries the archaizing legal register appropriate to this code.

**v.6 "avenger of blood."** Unanimous. *Go'el ha-dam* is preserved by all three. Consensus retains it throughout ch. 19 and beyond.

**v.9 "all the days" vs. "always."** EMET: "all the days." LOGOS/PNEUMA: "always." Consensus adopts "always" as the idiomatic rendering of the Hebrew *kol ha-yamim* in this context, which is temporal scope rather than enumerated days.

**v.13 "from Israel" vs. "from Yisra'el."** EMET uses transliterated "Yisra'el" throughout; LOGOS and PNEUMA use "Israel." Consensus uses "Israel" (English form) per project name conventions.

**v.16 "apostasy" (EMET) vs. "wrongdoing" (LOGOS) vs. "a crime" (PNEUMA).** The Hebrew *sarah* here is a technical legal term. EMET's "apostasy" carries too strong a theological freight for a general witness law; PNEUMA's "a crime" is too generic. LOGOS's "wrongdoing" best fits the legal-judicial context. Consensus follows LOGOS.

**v.19 "intended" (EMET/LOGOS) vs. "schemed" (PNEUMA).** Hebrew *zamam* = to plan, devise. "Intended" (EMET/LOGOS) is accurate and more neutral; "schemed" (PNEUMA) captures the premeditated malice. Consensus adopts "intended" to match the formal legal register of the passage.

**Vaticanus note.** Vaticanus p. 155 aligns with MT throughout. The five-fold lex talionis formula (v. 21) and the two/three-witness requirement (v. 15) are confirmed without addition or omission.

---

*Arbiter: SOPHIA*
